brandcolor-primary.usage: Main CTA button background, primary brand color

brandcolor-primaryhover.usage: Hover state for primary buttons (when background is primary)
whenToUse: Hover on elements with primary background
examples: Full primary button: bg-brandcolor-primary text-brandcolor-white hover:bg-brandcolor-primaryhover
brandcolor-primaryhover.logic: When a button or element has bg-brandcolor-primary, the hover should use bg-brandcolor-primaryhover (darker shade of primary)

brandcolor-secondary.usage: Secondary brand color for outline/secondary buttons with border
examples: Outline button: border-brandcolor-secondary text-brandcolor-secondary bg-transparent hover:bg-brandcolor-neutralhover

brandcolor-secondaryfill.usage: Brand color secondary fill – active/selected state background (e.g. Natural language dropdown when open)
whenToUse: Active state of secondary-related controls (e.g. search type dropdown when open)
Selected pill/badge backgrounds tied to secondary actions
Light fill where secondary (#2F55D9) is the accent

brandcolor-secondaryhover.usage: Hover state for secondary buttons (darker shade of secondary)
whenToUse: Hover on elements with secondary background
brandcolor-secondaryhover.logic: When a button has secondary background color, the hover should use bg-brandcolor-secondaryhover (#183885) which is a darker shade of secondary for better visual feedback.

brandcolor-neutralhover.usage: Hover state for neutral buttons and outline buttons (NOT bg-brandcolor-fill)
whenToUse: Hover on elements with neutral/outline style
brandcolor-neutralhover.logic: When a button is neutral or outline style (with border), the hover should use bg-brandcolor-neutralhover (#EFEFEF), NOT bg-brandcolor-fill. This is a lighter gray specifically for neutral and outline button hovers.

brandcolor-textstrong.usage: Headings, main headings, button text in some cases

brandcolor-textweak.usage: Secondary text, secondary hierarchy after main text

brandcolor-strokestrong.usage: ALWAYS use for: Sidebar borders, input field borders, button borders, icons
brandcolor-strokestrong.importantNote: ALWAYS use strokestrong for sidebar borders, input field borders, and button borders. This is a mandatory rule.

brandcolor-strokeweak.usage: ALWAYS use for: Card borders, dividers, and all other light elements
whenToUse: Secondary button borders (ALL secondary buttons use this border color - exception to button border rule)
brandcolor-strokeweak.importantNote: ALWAYS use strokeweak for card borders and dividers. Secondary buttons are an exception and also use strokeweak, but all other button borders use strokestrong.

brandcolor-strokemild.usage: ALWAYS use for: Table row borders when hovered
whenToUse: Table row borders when row is hovered (hover:border-brandcolor-strokemild)
brandcolor-strokemild.importantNote: ALWAYS use strokemild for table row borders on hover. Selected rows use border-brandcolor-secondary instead.

brandcolor-strokelight.usage: ALWAYS use for: Primary-outline button borders
whenToUse: Light stroke borders for primary-outline buttons
brandcolor-strokelight.importantNote: ALWAYS use strokelight for primary-outline button default border. Active state uses border-brandcolor-primary.

brandcolor-fill.usage: Layout background (NOT for neutral/outline button hover - use brandcolor-neutralhover instead)
brandcolor-fill.importantNote: DO NOT use this for neutral/outline button hover - use brandcolor-neutralhover (#EFEFEF) instead

brandcolor-white.usage: Background color in general wherever necessary

brandcolor-sidebarhover.usage: Hover state background color for sidebar menu items and dropdown menu backgrounds
brandcolor-sidebarhover.logic: Used specifically for sidebar navigation items when hovered and for dropdown menu backgrounds. Provides a darker background (#2E3C48) for better visual feedback on sidebar interactions and consistent dropdown styling.

whenToUse: Non-critical messages that relate to secondary actions
examples: Info banner: bg-brandcolor-banner-info-bg with secondary button using bg-brandcolor-secondary text-brandcolor-white

brandcolor-banner-warning-bg.usage: Background color for warning / alert banners
examples: Warning banner: bg-brandcolor-banner-warning-bg with warning button using bg-brandcolor-banner-warning-button text-brandcolor-white

brandcolor-banner-warning-button.usage: Button background color for warning banners
whenToUse: Primary action button inside warning banners
examples: Warning banner button: bg-brandcolor-banner-warning-button text-brandcolor-white rounded-md px-4 py-2

brandcolor-results-bg.usage: Background color for results layout and scrollable content areas

brandcolor-archived-bg.usage: Muted background for archived cards (distinct from view-only fill)
examples: Archived card: bg-brandcolor-archived-bg border-2 border-dashed border-brandcolor-archived-border opacity-70
Archived card border: border-2 border-dashed border-brandcolor-archived-border

brandcolor-archived-badge.usage: Background for 'Archived' badge/label on archived cards
examples: Archived badge: bg-brandcolor-archived-badge text-brandcolor-textweak px-2 py-1 rounded

brandcolor-destructive.usage: Destructive or archive action text (e.g. Archive menu item in project card dropdown)

brandcolor-table-header.usage: Table header background color (variant of strokeweak)

examples: Success badge: bg-brandcolor-badge-success-bg text-brandcolor-badge-success-text px-2 py-1 rounded

whenToUse: Attorney or similar entity card badge background

brandcolor-badge-attorney-text.usage: Attorney/entity card badge icon and label color (dark purple)
whenToUse: Attorney badge icon and label (text-strong weight)
Amber variant entity card badge background

brandcolor-badge-amber-text.usage: Amber entity card badge icon and label color

cornerRadius.description: ALL buttons (primary and secondary) MUST have 6px corner radius. Use rounded-md utility class.

secondaryBorderWidth.description: ALL secondary buttons (both types) MUST have 1.5px border width. Use border-[1.5px] utility class.

primary.description: Main CTA button with primary brand color

defaultState.logic: Uses primary brand color (#008B31) as background with white text. Has transparent border in default state to prevent layout shift when border appears on focus/active. ALWAYS includes rounded-md (6px corner radius).

hoverState.logic: On hover, background changes to brandcolor-primaryhover (#016323) which is a darker shade of primary for better visual feedback.

focusState.logic: On focus, transparent border changes to primary color (#008B31).

activeState.logic: On press/active: transparent border changes to primary color (#008B31), and inner shadow appears (inset 3px 3px 10px 0px rgba(0, 26, 49, 0.33)).

primary.completeClasses: border-[1.5px] border-transparent bg-brandcolor-primary text-brandcolor-white hover:bg-brandcolor-primaryhover focus:border-brandcolor-primary active:border-brandcolor-primary active:shadow-button-press disabled:opacity-50 rounded-md
primary.whenToUse: Use for main call-to-action buttons with primary brand color

neutral.description: Neutral button with textweak color and strokelight border

defaultState.logic: White background with strokelight border (#C5C5C5) at 1.5px width and textweak color text (#5C6166). ALWAYS includes rounded-md (6px corner radius).

hoverState.logic: On hover, background changes to brandcolor-neutralhover (#EFEFEF). Border and text color remain the same.

activeState.logic: On press/active: border changes to textweak color (#5C6166), text remains textweak color, and inner shadow appears (inset 3px 3px 10px 0px rgba(0, 26, 49, 0.33)).

disabledState.logic: Disabled state reduces background opacity to 50%.

neutral.completeClasses: border-[1.5px] border-brandcolor-strokelight text-brandcolor-textweak bg-brandcolor-white hover:bg-brandcolor-neutralhover active:border-brandcolor-textweak active:text-brandcolor-textweak active:shadow-button-press disabled:opacity-50 rounded-md
neutral.whenToUse: Use for neutral actions that don't require primary or secondary emphasis

card.description: Cards with proper borders and dynamic width
card.logic: Card borders MUST use strokeweak for subtle, light appearance.
criticalRules: ALWAYS use border border-brandcolor-strokeweak for card borders
ALWAYS use bg-brandcolor-white for card background
ALWAYS use rounded-lg for card border radius
Use shadow-card for consistent card elevation

h1.logic: Strong text color for main headings

h2.logic: Strong text color for secondary headings

h3.logic: Strong text color for tertiary headings

input.description: Input fields with proper borders - MUST override Flowbite and browser defaults
input.logic: Strong stroke border by default, changes to text-strong color on focus. No extra border/ring on focus. Text color is always text-strong. CRITICAL: Must use focus:ring-0 to prevent Flowbite's default ring styles. Must use focus:outline-none to prevent browser default outline.
criticalRules: ALWAYS include focus:ring-0 to override Flowbite's default focus ring
ALWAYS include focus:outline-none to override browser default outline
NEVER allow Flowbite or browser default focus styles
Focus border MUST be brandcolor-textstrong (#001A31), NEVER brandcolor-secondary or any other color

workflow: STEP 1 (MANDATORY): Read tailwind.config.js FIRST to see what theme colors are available
STEP 2 (MANDATORY): Read this theme-guide-v2.json file COMPLETELY to understand all rules
STEP 3: Check componentGuidelines section for the specific component you're creating
STEP 4: Generate code using ONLY theme utility classes from tailwind.config.js
STEP 5: Follow componentGuidelines exactly (requiredClasses, cornerRadius, etc.)
STEP 6: Verify no hex codes, no inline styles, no hardcoded Tailwind colors

noHexCodes.rule: NEVER use hex color codes (#008B31, #2F55D9, etc.) directly in HTML, CSS, or inline styles

noInlineStyles.rule: NEVER use inline styles (style='color: #008B31') or style attributes for colors

noHardcodedTailwindColors.rule: NEVER use hardcoded Tailwind color classes (bg-blue-700, text-gray-900, border-gray-200, etc.)

onlyUtilityClasses.rule: ONLY use utility classes from theme-guide-v2.json and tailwind.config.js

mistake1.correct: <button class="bg-brandcolor-primary text-brandcolor-white hover:bg-brandcolor-primaryhover rounded-md px-4 py-2">Button</button>
mistake1.reason: Never use hardcoded Tailwind colors. Always use theme classes with required classes.

mistake2.reason: Never use inline styles or hex codes. Always use utility classes.

mistake3.reason: All buttons MUST use rounded-md (6px), not rounded-lg or any other radius.
